title: Tech-investor med 27 pct. i gennemsnitligt afkast: Her er hans aktier
summary_md:
# Tech-investor med 27 pct. i gennemsnitligt afkast: Her er hans aktier

**Feed**: borsen-podcast  
**Published**: 2026-05-27  
**Market Sentiment**: Mixed

⏱️ **Time Horizon Key**: ST = <3 months | MT = 3-12 months | LT = >12 months

---

## Episode Thesis

The episode centers on SpaceX's record-breaking upcoming Nasdaq IPO (June 12), debating its sky-high valuation, Elon Musk's near-total voting control via super-voting B-shares, and the index-inclusion mechanics that force passive funds to buy. The hosts then profile the profitable-growth tech fund Horizon 3 (27% annualized return) and discuss conviction holdings across chips (Nvidia, Micron, AMD, ARM), software (ServiceNow), and big tech (Alphabet, Microsoft). Overall mood is enthusiastic on profitable AI/chip names but skeptical and cautious on SpaceX's unprofitable, governance-poor structure.

---

## Universe Stocks

### 🟢 NVDA — Nvidia | **BUY** (Strong)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 85%

Largest fund position; beat across the board with guidance raise, revenue +85% and earnings +130% at P/E 21; Rubin platform, CUDA moat and non-hyperscaler expansion to 250,000 customers.

#### Reasoning

Hosts call it 'storbror i klassen' and very attractive long-term. Despite huge run, it trades below consensus on a P/E of ~21 with 85% revenue growth; the Rubin generation uses more memory (also bullish for Micron), datacenter is 90%+ of revenue now split ~50/50 hyperscaler/non-hyperscaler, lowering concentration risk. Innovation pace keeps competitors at bay.

#### Sell Triggers

- if Rubin ramp compresses margins more than expected
- if hyperscaler capex slows
- if competitors close the CUDA software gap

> *"NVIDIA är den största det är faktiskt vår största position ... den handlar till en P på 21 och sådant ... vi syns den är riktigt attraktiv för det långsiktiga"*

---

### 🟢 MU — Micron | **BUY** (Strong)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 75%

Third-largest position; +214% YTD, +19% on UBS note implying 82% upside; trades at P/E ~8-15; hosts believe the memory upcycle is structural not merely cyclical.

#### Reasoning

After a fenomenal report it dipped, then rallied; the key debate is cyclical vs structural and the fund leans structural given Rubin's higher memory content and bottleneck economics. At a forward P/E of ~8-15 with strong earnings growth, risk-reward is favorable; they have already taken profit so the remaining position is effectively de-risked. UBS sees a fair value well above current price.

#### Sell Triggers

- if memory pricing proves cyclical and rolls over (back toward 300)
- if unit volume growth fails to follow price gains
- if AI capex cycle peaks

> *"i håller den ni tror på strukturell växt det är inte cykliskt ... vi har också tagit gevinst hem på den ... den position vi har nu har vi faktiskt betalt för"*

---

### 🟢 ARM — ARM | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 65%

+200% YTD; energy-efficient licensing model expanding from mobile into datacenters; own chip via Meta but only launches 2028 (execution risk); position raised to ~0.5% of fund.

#### Reasoning

Henrik calls it a super-exciting business and added on the sideways base since 'Rose Garden'; even after a big move it can run further on compute-power growth and energy efficiency. Not cheap (forward P/E ~145, down from 450) and there is execution risk on the first in-house chip in 2028, but the long thesis on energy-efficient compute is intact.

#### Sell Triggers

- if the 2028 own-chip launch slips or fails
- if datacenter adoption stalls
- if valuation (P/E ~145) compresses on a growth miss

> *"ARM syns vi är en superspännande verksamhet ... även om det är stigit mycket kan det stigit mycket ännu"*

---

### 🟡 AMD — AMD | **HOLD** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Held in the fund; hyperscalers increasingly buy AMD alongside Nvidia; cited as a position that exploded after a long base-building period, underscoring patience.

#### Reasoning

AMD is named among the top chip holdings (Micron, AMD, Alphabet) and benefits from the same AI compute tailwind, with hyperscalers diversifying suppliers toward it. The discussion frames it as a long-term hold where timing the breakout is hard, rewarding staying invested.

#### Sell Triggers

- if hyperscaler diversification away from Nvidia stalls
- if AI capex cycle peaks

> *"cloud-leverantörerna köper från NVIDIA och nu i stigande grad också från AMD"*

---

### 🟢 GOOGL — Alphabet (Google) | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 70%

Top holding in both the fund and the All-Star portfolio; earlier search-migration fears proved wrong and the stock has rerated; among the largest chip/tech positions.

#### Reasoning

The fund was briefly nervous about users shifting search to Anthropic/GPT and its impact on ad revenue, but concedes that fear was misplaced and they are comfortably invested. It is held in both portfolios and named among their largest positions (Micron, AMD, Alphabet), reflecting renewed conviction.

#### Sell Triggers

- if AI assistants materially erode search ad revenue
- if regulatory action breaks up the ad business

> *"vi var lite nervösa för ... mer och mer det flydde till Antropic och till ett GPT ... men där må vi bara konstatera att det är fint att ta fel ... vi är fint inne i den nu"*

---

### 🟢 MSFT — Microsoft | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 65%

Category-1 holding sized up to ~9%; fell on recent results despite delivering, now seen as better value at P/E 22 vs 30; 25bn USD quarterly capex on AI hardware.

#### Reasoning

Hosts view Microsoft as a good business that is even more attractive after derating: 'good at P/E 30, even better at P/E 22' for a multi-year hold. It is the only hyperscaler disclosing OpenAI contribution, gets punished for transparency, but fundamentals and AI capex remain strong.

#### Sell Triggers

- if software demand cannot keep up with capex
- if AI monetization disappoints
- if margins compress from hardware price inflation

> *"vi tycker att om den var god till en P på 30 så tycker vi att den är ännu bättre till en P på 22 om vi ska ha den i många år"*

---

### 🟢 NOW — ServiceNow | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 65%

Workflow-automation platform down 31% YTD despite beats; Now Assist AI revenue guidance raised to 1.5bn USD; 22 years of workflow data as a moat; turnaround candidate.

#### Reasoning

The fund finds it interesting now: mission-critical workflows with low fault tolerance and high regulatory barriers protect incumbents from 'vibe-coded' AI replacements. Now Assist consumption-based AI revenue is accelerating (raised from 1bn to 1.5bn USD), investor-day targets imply revenue doubling by 2030 with Rule-of-40 >60 and 98% retention, while the stock has gone nowhere in 5 years.

#### Sell Triggers

- if seat-based license growth keeps decelerating without consumption offset
- if AI agents commoditize workflow orchestration
- if Now Assist revenue ramp stalls

> *"den aktie som heter ServiceNow ... den är ju ramt i år den är nere med 31% ... där har de uppjusterat ... till 1,5"*

---

### 🟡 SPOT — Spotify | **HOLD** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Category-2 digital-platform holding; grows revenue without increasing headcount, cited as a strong AI-driven efficiency story among digitally-born businesses.

#### Reasoning

Spotify is highlighted as a digitally-born company benefiting from AI as a huge efficiency driver — doing much more for the same or less — which supports margins and is a key reason the fund holds it in the platform theme.

#### Sell Triggers

- if subscriber growth stalls
- if margin gains from efficiency plateau

> *"Spotify som växer men som inte ökar omsättningen eller antalet headcounts för de kan göra mycket mer för mindre"*

---

### ⛔ TSLA — Tesla | **AVOID** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Mixed | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 50%

Henrik sold his holding at a profit after the US election, citing unclear focus and Musk launching his own party; trades at high multiples on negative growth; possible SpaceX merger.

#### Reasoning

The fund exited after the election over brand/strategy concerns and high P/E on negative growth that doesn't fit their risk profile; they won't own it now but also won't short it. A potential merger into SpaceX adds uncertainty. Stance is steer-clear rather than active short.

#### Sell Triggers

- if growth and margins recover
- if a SpaceX merger is structured favorably for Tesla holders

> *"vi sålde vår aktie med ett fint profit efter valet ... vi vill inte investera i hamn för öjeblicket men vi vill heller inte shorta hamn"*

---
